title: Intro to basics
summary:
Take your first steps with R. In this chapter, you will learn how to
use the console as a calculator and how to assign variables. You
will also get to know the basic data types in R. Let's get started.

title: Vectors
summary:
We take you on a trip to Vegas, where you will learn how to analyze
your gambling results using vectors in R. After completing this
chapter, you will be able to create vectors in R, name them, select
elements from them, and compare different vectors.

title: Matrices
summary:
In this chapter, you will learn how to work with matrices in R. By
the end of the chapter, you will be able to create matrices and
understand how to do basic computations with them. You will analyze
the box office numbers of the Star Wars movies and learn how to use
matrices in R. May the force be with you!

title: Factors
summary:
Data often falls into a limited number of categories. For example,
human hair color can be categorized as black, brown, blond, red,
grey, or white—and perhaps a few more options for people who color
their hair. In R, categorical data is stored in factors. Factors are
very important in data analysis, so start learning how to create,
subset, and compare them now.

title: Data frames
summary:
Most datasets you will be working with will be stored as data
frames. By the end of this chapter, you will be able to create a
data frame, select interesting parts of a data frame, and order a
data frame according to certain variables.

title: Lists
summary:
As opposed to vectors, lists can hold components of different types,
just as your to-do lists can contain different categories of tasks.
This chapter will teach you how to create, name, and subset these
lists.

title: Overview
summary: Master the basics of data analysis with Python in just four hours. This online course will introduce the Python interface and explore popular packages.

title: An Introduction to Python
summary: Python has grown to become the market leader in programming languages and the language of choice for data analysts and data scientists. Demand for data skills is rising because companies want to gain actionable insights from their data.

title: Discover the Python Basics
summary: This is a Python course for beginners, and we designed it for people with no prior Python experience. It is even suitable if you have no coding experience at all. You will cover the basics of Python, helping you understand common, everyday functions and applications, including how to use Python as a calculator, understanding variables and types, and building Python lists. The first half of this course prepares you to use Python interactively and teaches you how to store, access, and manipulate data using one of the most popular programming languages in the world.

title: Explore Python Functions and Packages
summary: The second half of the course starts with a view of how you can use functions, methods, and packages to use code that other Python developers have written. As an open-source language, Python has plenty of existing packages and libraries that you can use to solve your problems.

title: Get Started with NumPy
summary: NumPy is an essential Python package for data science. You’ll finish this course by learning to use some of the most popular tools in the NumPy array and start exploring data in Python.

title: Introduction to Statistics in R
summary:
Grow your statistical skills and learn how to collect, analyze, and
draw accurate conclusions from data.

title: Foundations of Probability in R
summary:
In this course, you'll learn about the concepts of random variables,
distributions, and conditioning.

title: Introduction to Regression in R
summary:
Predict housing prices and ad click-through rate by implementing,
analyzing, and interpreting regression analysis in R.

title: Intermediate Regression in R
summary:
Learn to perform linear and logistic regression with multiple
explanatory variables.

title: Sampling in R
summary: Master sampling to get more accurate statistics with less data.

title: Hypothesis Testing in R
summary:
Learn how and when to use hypothesis testing in R, including
t-tests, proportion tests, and chi-square tests.

title: Experimental Design in R
summary:
In this course you'll learn about basic experimental design, a
crucial part of any data analysis.

title: Analyzing Survey Data in R
summary:
Learn survey design using common design structures followed by
visualizing and analyzing survey results.

title: Hierarchical and Mixed Effects Models in R
summary:
In this course you will learn to fit hierarchical models with random
effects.

title: Survival Analysis in R
summary:
Learn to work with time-to-event data. The event may be death or
finding a job after unemployment. Learn to estimate, visualize, and
interpret survival models!

title: Fundamentals of Bayesian Data Analysis in R
summary:
Learn what Bayesian data analysis is, how it works, and why it is a
useful tool to have in your data science toolbox.

title: Factor Analysis in R
summary:
Explore latent variables, such as personality, using exploratory and
confirmatory factor analyses.

title: Foundations of Inference in R
summary:
Learn how to draw conclusions about a population from a sample of
data via a process known as statistical inference.

title: How Environmental Factors Influence Wildlife Populations
